item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: really enjoyed reading this!!! this is such a minor thing but it’s hard for me to picture how big it actually is? size specifications would be cool and helpful. great writing tho +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Made some revisions o/ Followed the crit and fixed some typos and awkward wording and excised mention of -2

Also completely overhauled the history section, in part to explain why the guy doing the trials wasn't found out, in part to make pointed commentary on how those in power still desire even more and even if they can be 'nice' they'll ultimately sacrifice the weak in order to seize that excess desire

it be like that sometimes

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Alright, finished up with school stuff, can now proceed to talk a bit more about the SCP! First off, I will be doing a bit of a bit of revisioning once JamCon ends. The article was written in a rush because JamCon so some things are a little rushed, could stand to be cleaned up a bit (the history section in particular was /really/ last minute and could stand to be a bit more cohesive and punchier)

The main intent with this article an object that compelled you to do horrible things not through some memetic/compulsive/mind affecting in general effect, but through application of psychological principles.

For example, people are more inclined to do further somethings for someone (and begin to regard them as trustworthy and good) if they do even a small thing for that someone first. The Benjamin Franklin effect, since the lad used it to make political opponents come to like him @ having them do small favors for him. That's why the first trial, walking a thousand miles, is something you'll complete/do for the box even if you don't want to. You literally can't help but starting the trials, you literally can't help but doing something for the box. From there, it has a wedge in you, and increasingly uses various means of reinforcement to keep you doing the trials "you can't stop now you've already started" "what's one more" "I believe in you" "what you've done will be meaningless if you don't keep going"

It's the exact sort of stuff that can make cults commit mass suicide irl @ refuse to believe in reality and further and further justify their delusions because they've sacrificed/come this far/need to finish what they've started/have been positively reinforced etc., so it's actually entirely realistic a guy would go this far for an object that's shown itself to have some anomalous properties

Sidewindered and FuelBoy Thank you very much for the positive comments ! They mean a lot !

ModernMajorGeneral Thank you greatly for the crit; I'll be fixing that one error, and I definitely might change the shark punching ref ! I'll also definitely take the order of description into consideration.

Funnily enough, it's not actually /cursed/ in one sense. It genuinely would have granted the guy's super conscious desire, it's just a stupid ass in one sense box for all its psychological manipulation and it genuinely innocently prioritized the guy's hongry over his conscious wants. If he hadn't been hongry it might have all been worth it; the senselessness of it all was why I felt real Shaggy dog hours.

Shaggydredlocks Thank you so much for the praise and I'm glad you enjoyed reading ! That honestly is a pretty good idea and I'll definitely look into it for the revision !

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was a really fun read, and the trials were excellently written. I think your ending would have a much stronger punch though, if you were to excise all the mentions of -2.

item.title: Mmmm....chesborger
item.content:encoded: In all seriousness, this is a pretty good article. The subtle increase in serverity as the trial go up along with the tone of the trial messages remaining the same kept me engaged. +1 and a cheeseburger.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Minor issues:
- if you were really pedantic you could argue this isn't really a shaggy dog story
- it annoys me when the circumstances of the appearance of SCP-3819-1 are described before describing what SCP-3819 is
- "SCP-3819-1 is standard sheet of"
- the shark punching reference is weirdly out of place

Cursed wish granter is not a very original idea (how many twilight zone episodes did this item appear in?) but I thought this was still entertaining, as I'm a sucker for the 'terrifying evil thing that turns out to be a bit shit' brand of comedy, so +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Second ever SCP

Source: https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/4/4a/Image_larnax_of_philip.jpg

Creative commons and whatnot

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QEOy4zEVFoU

I'll talk more later I actually forgot to make this post before somebody else commented and now it's awkward

(I'm glad you liked it though thank you!)
